name: Thomas Wolf
bio: Thomas Wolf is co-founder and Chief Science Officer (CSO) of Hugging Face, the collaborative open-source platform for machine learning that hosts over 1 million public models and is valued at $4.5 billion. After graduating from Ecole Polytechnique (Paris), he researched laser-plasma interactions at the BELLA Center of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. He completed his Ph.D. in Statistical/Quantum Physics at Sorbonne University and ESPCI, working on superconducting materials. He then pivoted to law, earning a degree from Pantheon Sorbonne University and spending five years as a European patent attorney at Cabinet Plasseraud. In 2015, consulting for deep-learning startups, he recognized that many AI methods were re-branded statistical physics approaches and taught himself modern ML. In 2016, alongside Clement Delangue and Julien Chaumond, he co-founded Hugging Face in New York City, initially as a chatbot app before pivoting to become the central hub for open-source AI. Wolf created the Transformers library (158k+ GitHub stars) and the Datasets library, co-authored the O'Reilly book 'Natural Language Processing with Transformers,' initiated and led the BigScience research workshop that produced BLOOM (a 176-billion-parameter multilingual LLM), and now leads Hugging Face's push into open-source robotics with LeRobot. His papers have been cited over 55,000 times.

timeline.event: Completed Ph.D. in Statistical/Quantum Physics at Sorbonne University and ESPCI, Paris
Co-founded Hugging Face with Clement Delangue and Julien Chaumond in New York City
Open-sourced PyTorch pretrained BERT, sparking what became the Transformers library
Co-authored DistilBERT paper (NeurIPS 2019) — smaller, faster, cheaper BERT distillation
Hugging Face raised $15M Series A led by Lux Capital
Published 'Transformers: State-of-the-art NLP' — won EMNLP 2020 Best Demo Paper
Published 'Datasets: A Community Library for NLP' — won EMNLP 2021 Best Demo Paper
Launched BigScience research workshop — largest open collaboration in AI with 1,000+ researchers
Co-authored 'Natural Language Processing with Transformers' (O'Reilly, with Tunstall & von Werra)
BigScience released BLOOM — 176B-parameter open multilingual LLM trained on 46 languages
Hugging Face raised $235M Series D at $4.5B valuation from Google, Nvidia, Amazon, Salesforce
Launched LeRobot — open-source robotics library bringing the Hugging Face community approach to physical AI
Co-authored FineWeb dataset paper — 15-trillion-token open pretraining dataset from Common Crawl
Released SmolLM family of small language models (135M to 1.7B) with open training corpus
Fortune interview: advocated that open-source AI benefits far outweigh risks
Spoke at MLOps Community 'Agents in Production' event on LLMs, agents and open-source
Released FineWeb2 — multilingual web dataset spanning 3 trillion words across many languages
Spoke at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025 on building the future of open AI
Appeared on Sequoia's 'Training Data' podcast discussing LeRobot and the 'App Store for Robots'

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Transformers
key_contributions.description: Created the Transformers library (158k+ GitHub stars), providing a unified API for state-of-the-art pretrained models across PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX. Used by 5,000+ research organizations worldwide.

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Datasets
key_contributions.description: Created the Datasets library for efficient access to thousands of ML datasets. Won EMNLP 2021 Best Demonstration Paper.

key_contributions.title: BigScience / BLOOM
key_contributions.description: Initiated and led BigScience, the largest open research collaboration in AI (1,000+ researchers, 60+ countries). Produced BLOOM, a 176B-parameter multilingual open LLM trained on 46 languages.

key_contributions.title: DistilBERT
key_contributions.description: Co-authored DistilBERT (NeurIPS 2019), demonstrating that a 40% smaller BERT model retaining 97% of performance could be produced via knowledge distillation, enabling wider deployment.

key_contributions.title: Natural Language Processing with Transformers (O'Reilly)
key_contributions.description: Co-authored the reference book on building language applications with Hugging Face, published by O'Reilly (2022).

key_contributions.title: LeRobot
key_contributions.description: Leading Hugging Face's open-source robotics initiative, bringing community-driven development to physical AI with affordable hardware like the $100 SO100 robotic arm.

key_contributions.title: FineWeb / FineWeb2
key_contributions.description: Co-authored FineWeb (15T tokens) and FineWeb2 (3T+ words, multilingual) — open pretraining datasets that produce better-performing LLMs than other open data sources.

key_contributions.title: SmolLM
key_contributions.description: Led release of SmolLM family of small language models (135M-1.7B params) with fully open training data, demonstrating that small open models can rival larger proprietary ones.

quotes.text: It's nice to give a fish to someone to feed them, it's even better to teach them to fish.
Everyone feels like they can build with AI and not just consuming AI.
All of these people also become roboticists in a way, if you give them the tools.
The missing brick was really software that could adapt, that could be dynamic.
The big bet was, can you build a big community in robotics as well?

sources.title: Open-source AI benefits outweigh risks (Fortune, Oct 2024)
Hugging Face's Thomas Wolf on the 'App Store' for Robots (Sequoia)
The Past, Present and Future of Hugging Face - Analytics Vidhya
